TORNADO BEGAN APPROXIMATELY 1.5 MILES WEST-SOUTHWEST OF DELL AND MOVED NORTHEAST ALONG HIGHWAY 18...EVENTUALLY LIFTING 4 MILES NORTHEAST OF DELL. PATH LENGTH WAS 5.6 MILES WITH A MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH OF 150 YARDS. TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND FOR APPROXIMATELY 6 MINUTES AND WAS RATED EF1. A MOBILE HOME WAS DESTROYED AND SEVERAL BUILDINGS AT A COTTON GIN WERE DAMAGED. DAMAGE TO TREES AND ROOFS WAS ALSO OBSERVED.

AN APARTMENT COMPLEX SUSTAINED ROOF DAMAGE AND SEVERAL WINDOWS WERE ALSO BLOWN OUT. IN ADDITION...FENCE AND SIDING DEBRIS WERE LOFTED AND SCATTERED ABOUT THE AREA. THIS TORNADO WAS RATED EF-1.

A BRIEF TORNADO OCCURRED IN SOUTHWEST CARROLL COUNTY WEDNESDAY EVENING BETWEEN THE COMMUNITIES OF HUNTINGDON AND CEDAR GROVE. THIS TORNADO PRODUCED WIDESPREAD TREE DAMAGE...MAINLY ALONG AND EAST OF HIGHWAY 70 FROM HIGHWAY 424 NORTH TO WATER TOWER ROAD. IN ADDITION TO THE TREE DAMAGE...SEVERAL HOMES AND SINGLE WIDE MOBILE HOMES SUSTAINED DAMAGE...PRIMARILY TO ROOF STRUCTURES. IN ALL APPROXIMATELY 15 TO 20 BUILDINGS AND OUTBUILDINGS WERE DAMAGED BY THIS TORNADO.
